content:
Most people think prompt engineering is a mysterious skill. It’s actually one simple framework — and once you see it, you can’t unsee it.

3 min read
Just now

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

There’s a reason two people can type into the exact same AI model and get completely different quality of output. It has nothing to do with which AI they’re using. It comes down to one thing: how the prompt is structured.
Once you understand this, you stop guessing and start getting consistent, professional-grade results from ChatGPT, Claude, or Gemini every single time.
The CRAFT Framework
Every strong prompt — whether it’s for writing, coding, marketing, or research — follows the same underlying structure:
Context — What background does the AI need to understand the situation?
Role — Who should the AI “become” while answering? A copywriter? A senior developer? A career coach?
Action — What exactly do you want done? Be specific, not vague.
Format — How should the output be structured? Bullet points? A table? A specific word count?
Tone — What voice and style should it use? Formal? Conversational? Persuasive?
Miss even one of these and the AI fills in the blanks with generic assumptions — which is exactly why most people’s prompts produce forgettable results.
Seeing the Difference in Practice
Here’s a prompt without structure:
“Help me write a cold email.”
Here’s the same request run through CRAFT:
“Write a 5-email cold outreach sequence for [PRODUCT] targeting [ROLE] at [COMPANY TYPE]. Goal: [GOAL].”
The second version gives the AI a role, a scope, a format (5 emails, sequenced), and a defined outcome. That’s the entire difference between output that sounds like a template and output that sounds like it was written by someone who actually understands your business.
Why I Built a 500+ Prompt Library Around This
Once I recognized the pattern, I realized most people don’t need to learn prompt engineering from scratch — they just need a library of prompts that already apply it correctly, so they can copy, customize, and go.
That’s what became the AI Prompts Mega Vault — a free bundle of over 500 prompts, all built on the CRAFT framework, spanning:

• Writing & storytelling

• Marketing & copywriting

• Coding & technical tasks

• Business & strategy

• Education & learning

• Productivity & organization

• Research & analysis

• Social media & content

• Career & professional development

• 125+ cinematic AI video prompts for Sora, Veo, Runway, and Pika
Every prompt includes fillable [VARIABLE] fields, so applying the framework takes seconds instead of requiring you to write structured prompts from scratch.
Why AI Video Prompts Need Even More Structure
If you’ve tried generating video with Sora or Runway and gotten disappointing results, it’s usually because video prompts need an additional layer beyond CRAFT — they need technical cinematography language: lens type, camera movement, lighting setup, and pacing.
A weak video prompt: “A person walking on a beach at sunset.”
A structured one: “A person walks along a beach during golden hour, shot with an 85mm lens at f/1.8 for shallow depth of field, slow tracking camera movement, warm backlit rim lighting, cinematic slow-motion at 0.5x, contemplative atmosphere.”
The second version gives the AI model actual creative direction instead of leaving everything to chance — which is why the video vault inside the bundle focuses so heavily on precise technical vocabulary.
Who This Helps Most

• Anyone who’s used ChatGPT a handful of times and felt like the output was “just okay”

• Marketers and freelancers who need consistent, professional-quality copy fast

• Developers who want AI to genuinely assist with debugging and documentation

• Founders and consultants building pitch decks and strategy documents

• Creators experimenting with AI video generation for the first time
Get the Full Framework in Action
You don’t need to memorize the CRAFT method or write structured prompts from scratch. The Mega Vault already applies it across 500+ ready-to-use prompts.
👉 Download the AI Prompts Mega Vault — Free
If this shifted how you think about prompting, save the article and share it with someone who’s still typing one-line requests into ChatGPT.
